status_detail: Demonstration of Ceres Power's SOEC technology at partner facilities in Germany. MW-scale SOEC stack operating since 2024. Ceres' SteelCell technology operating in electrolysis mode (SOEC) producing hydrogen at >85% electrical efficiency. Targeted at industrial hydrogen applications including steel and ammonia.

data_management.narrative_summary: Ceres Power is a UK-based developer of solid oxide cell (SOC) technology that can operate in both fuel cell (SOFC) and electrolysis (SOEC) modes. The company's HoE (Hydrogen of Electrolysis) demonstration program operates MW-scale SOEC stacks at partner facilities in Germany, demonstrating the technology's ability to produce hydrogen at over 85% electrical efficiency by utilizing high-temperature steam. Ceres' SteelCell technology, based on metal-supported cells, is designed for cost-effective manufacturing using standard steel industry supply chains. The technology is being targeted at industrial-scale hydrogen production for steelmaking (direct injection into DRI furnaces) and ammonia production.

sources.title: Ceres Power — Green hydrogen from SOEC technology: HoE demo program
sources.author: Ceres Power / Bosch
